revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:霧島六社権現]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:霧島六社権現]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:霧島六社権現]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701}}; {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11660872]] Wikidata name: Kirishima rokusha gongen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11660872]] Wikidata name: Kirishima rokusha gongen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kirishima Rokusha Gongen''' (霧島六社権現) refers to a collective of six {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrines}} located around {{ill|Mount Kirishima|en|Mount Kirishima|WD=Q1319361|lt=Mount Kirishima}}, straddling the border between {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture}} and {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}. It is also known as '''Kirishima Rokusho Gongen''' (霧島六所権現).
* {{ill|Kirishima Mine Shrine|ja|霧島岑神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirishima Mine Shrine|1=Kirishimamine Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660876}} (Kirishima Mine Central Rokusho Gongen; affiliated temple: Setaoji) – Located in {{ill|Kobayashi, Miyazaki|en|Kobayashi, Miyazaki|WD=Q861283|lt=Kobayashi, Miyazaki}}, formerly a prefectural shrine. * '''Ebisumori Shrine''' (Ebisumori Rokusho Gongen; affiliated temple: {{ill|Hōkō-in (Kobayashi)|ja|宝光院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkō-in (Kobayashi)|1=houkouin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452337}}) – Also in Kobayashi, Miyazaki; merged with Kirishima Mine Shrine during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}. * {{ill|Sano Shrine|ja|狭野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Shrine|qq=}} (Sano Gongen; affiliated temple: Jintoku-in) – Located in {{ill|Takaharu, Miyazaki|en|Takaharu, Miyazaki|WD=Q1206617|lt=Takaharu, Miyazaki}}, formerly a prefectural shrine. Designated as a branch shrine of {{ill|Miyazaki Jingū|en|Miyazaki Jingū|WD=Q704686|lt=Miyazaki Jingū}} in 1915, later regaining independence and listed as a special shrine under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines}} in 1976. * Higashi Kirishima Shrine (Higashi Kirishima Gongen; affiliated temple: Chokushō-in) – Located in {{ill|Miyakonojō|en|Miyakonojō|WD=Q638301|lt=Miyakonojo, Miyazaki}}, formerly a prefectural shrine. * Kirishima Higashi Shrine (Kirishima Higashi Gozaisho Gongen; affiliated temple: Shakujō-in) – Located in Takaharu, Miyazaki; formerly a village shrine. * {{ill|Kirishima Shrine|en|Kirishima Shrine|WD=Q704622|lt=Kirishima Shrine}} (Kirishima Nishi Gozaisho Gongen; affiliated temple: Karin-ji) – Located in {{ill|Kirishima, Kagoshima|en|Kirishima, Kagoshima|WD=Q858352|lt=Kirishima, Kagoshima}}, formerly an imperial shrine.
The Kirishima Rokusha Gongen was organized by the monk {{ill|Shōkū|ja|性空|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkū|1=Shōkū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491623}}, who practiced {{ill|Shugendō|en|Shugendō|WD=Q1483875|lt=Shugendō}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Murakami|en|Emperor Murakami|WD=Q374015|lt=Emperor Murakami}}. Although these shrines currently enshrine deities from Japanese mythology, particularly the {{ill|Three Generations of Hyuga|ja|日向三代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Generations of Hyuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Three generations of Hyuga|WD=Q56347075}}, their origins lie in ancient {{ill|Mountain worship|en|Mountain worship|WD=Q93697439|lt=mountain worship}}, with Mount Kirishima itself revered as sacred. The area served as a center for Shugendō practitioners.
In addition to the six shrines listed above, some sources include {{ill|Shiratori Shrine (Ebino)|ja|白鳥神社 (えびの市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Shrine (Ebino)|1=Shiratori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580591}} in {{ill|Ebino, Miyazaki|en|Ebino, Miyazaki|WD=Q374701|lt=Ebino, Miyazaki}}. The ''{{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangoku Meishō Zue|1=Sangoku meisho zue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354726}}'' mentions '''Hanamai Rokusho Gongen''' in {{ill|Miyakonojō|en|Miyakonojō|WD=Q638301|lt=Miyakonojo}} and '''Yasuhara Kirishima Daigongen (Naka Kirishima Gongen)''' as part of the Kirishima Rokusha Gongen. This suggests that the grouping may have varied over time, reflecting the prominence of different shrines around Mount Kirishima<ref>Akihiko Kuroiwa, "Kirishima Rokusho Gongen" in ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Records,'' Miyazaki Prefectural Shrine Office, 1988.</ref>.
== Gallery == (From ''Sangoku Meishō Zue'') <gallery mode="packed"> File:三国名勝図会 西御在所社頭図.jpg|Kirishima Nishi Gozaisho Gongen Shrine File:三国名勝図会 雛守権現社.jpg|Hinamori Gongen Shrine File:三国名勝図会 霧島中央権現宮.jpg|Kirishima Chūō Gongen Shrine </gallery> <gallery mode="packed"> File:三国名勝図会 狭野大権現社.jpg|Sano Gongen Shrine File:三国名勝図会 霧島東御在所権現社.jpg|Kirishima Higashi Gozaisho Gongen Shrine File:三国名勝図会 東霧島権現社.jpg|Higashi Kirishima Gongen Shrine </gallery>

The Kirishima Rokusha Gongen was organized by the monk {{ill|Shōkū|ja|性空|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkū|1=Shōkū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491623}}, who practiced {{ill|Shugendō|en|Shugendō|WD=Q1483875|lt=Shugendō}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Murakami|en|Emperor Murakami|WD=Q374015|lt=Emperor Murakami}}. '''Kirishima Rokusha Gongen''' (霧島六社権現) refers to a collective of six {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrines}} located around {{ill|Mount Kirishima|en|Mount Kirishima|WD=Q1319361|lt=Mount Kirishima}}, straddling the border between {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture}} and {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}. It is also known as '''Kirishima Rokusho Gongen''' (霧島六所権現).
* {{ill|Kirishima Mine Shrine|ja|霧島岑神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirishima Mine Shrine|1=Kirishimamine Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660876}} (Kirishima Mine Central Rokusho Gongen; affiliated temple: Setaoji) – Located in {{ill|Kobayashi, Miyazaki|en|Kobayashi, Miyazaki|WD=Q861283|lt=Kobayashi, Miyazaki}}, formerly a prefectural shrine. * '''Ebisumori Shrine''' (Ebisumori Rokusho Gongen; affiliated temple: {{ill|Hōkō-in (Kobayashi)|ja|宝光院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkō-in (Kobayashi)|1=houkouin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452337}}) – Also in Kobayashi, Miyazaki; merged with Kirishima Mine Shrine during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}. * {{ill|Sano Shrine|ja|狭野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Shrine|qq=}} (Sano Gongen; affiliated temple: Jintoku-in) – Located in {{ill|Takaharu, Miyazaki|en|Takaharu, Miyazaki|WD=Q1206617|lt=Takaharu, Miyazaki}}, formerly a prefectural shrine. Designated as a branch shrine of {{ill|Miyazaki Jingū|en|Miyazaki Jingū|WD=Q704686|lt=Miyazaki Jingū}} in 1915, later regaining independence and listed as a special shrine under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines}} in 1976. * Higashi Kirishima Shrine (Higashi Kirishima Gongen; affiliated temple: Chokushō-in) – Located in {{ill|Miyakonojō|en|Miyakonojō|WD=Q638301|lt=Miyakonojo, Miyazaki}}, formerly a prefectural shrine. * Kirishima Higashi Shrine (Kirishima Higashi Gozaisho Gongen; affiliated temple: Shakujō-in) – Located in Takaharu, Miyazaki; formerly a village shrine. * {{ill|Kirishima Shrine|en|Kirishima Shrine|WD=Q704622|lt=Kirishima Shrine}} (Kirishima Nishi Gozaisho Gongen; affiliated temple: Karin-ji) – Located in {{ill|Kirishima, Kagoshima|en|Kirishima, Kagoshima|WD=Q858352|lt=Kirishima, Kagoshima}}, formerly an imperial shrine.
The Kirishima Rokusha Gongen was organized by the monk {{ill|Shōkū|ja|性空|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkū|1=Shōkū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491623}}, who practiced {{ill|Shugendō|en|Shugendō|WD=Q1483875|lt=Shugendō}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Murakami|en|Emperor Murakami|WD=Q374015|lt=Emperor Murakami}}. Although these shrines currently enshrine deities from Japanese mythology, particularly the {{ill|Three Generations of Hyuga|ja|日向三代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Generations of Hyuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Three generations of Hyuga|WD=Q56347075}}, their origins lie in ancient {{ill|Mountain worship|en|Mountain worship|WD=Q93697439|lt=mountain worship}}, with Mount Kirishima itself revered as sacred. The area served as a center for Shugendō practitioners.
In addition to the six shrines listed above, some sources include {{ill|Shiratori Shrine (Ebino)|ja|白鳥神社 (えびの市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Shrine (Ebino)|1=Shiratori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580591}} in {{ill|Ebino, Miyazaki|en|Ebino, Miyazaki|WD=Q374701|lt=Ebino, Miyazaki}}. The ''{{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangoku Meishō Zue|1=Sangoku meisho zue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354726}}'' mentions '''Hanamai Rokusho Gongen''' in {{ill|Miyakonojō|en|Miyakonojō|WD=Q638301|lt=Miyakonojo}} and '''Yasuhara Kirishima Daigongen (Naka Kirishima Gongen)''' as part of the Kirishima Rokusha Gongen. This suggests that the grouping may have varied over time, reflecting the prominence of different shrines around Mount Kirishima<ref>Akihiko Kuroiwa, "Kirishima Rokusho Gongen" in ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Records,'' Miyazaki Prefectural Shrine Office, 1988.</ref>.
== Gallery == (From ''Sangoku Meishō Zue'') <gallery mode="packed"> File:三国名勝図会 西御在所社頭図.jpg|Kirishima Nishi Gozaisho Gongen Shrine File:三国名勝図会 雛守権現社.jpg|Hinamori Gongen Shrine File:三国名勝図会 霧島中央権現宮.jpg|Kirishima Chūō Gongen Shrine </gallery> <gallery mode="packed"> File:三国名勝図会 狭野大権現社.jpg|Sano Gongen Shrine File:三国名勝図会 霧島東御在所権現社.jpg|Kirishima Higashi Gozaisho Gongen Shrine File:三国名勝図会 東霧島権現社.jpg|Higashi Kirishima Gongen Shrine </gallery>

In addition to the six shrines listed above, some sources include {{ill|Shiratori Shrine (Ebino)|ja|白鳥神社 (えびの市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Shrine (Ebino)|1=Shiratori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580591}} in {{ill|Ebino, Miyazaki|en|Ebino, Miyazaki|WD=Q374701|lt=Ebino, Miyazaki|1=Ebino, Miyazaki}}. The ''{{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangoku Meishō Zue|1=Sangoku meisho zue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354726}}'' mentions '''Hanamai Rokusho Gongen''' in {{ill|Miyakonojō|en|Miyakonojō|WD=Q638301|lt=Miyakonojo|1=Miyakonojō}} and '''Yasuhara Kirishima Daigongen (Naka Kirishima Gongen)''' as part of the Kirishima Rokusha Gongen. This suggests that the grouping may have varied over time, reflecting the prominence of different shrines around Mount Kirishima<ref>Akihiko Kuroiwa, "Kirishima Rokusho Gongen" in ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Records,'' Miyazaki Prefectural Shrine Office, 1988.</ref>.
== Gallery == (From ''Sangoku Meishō Zue'') <gallery mode="packed"> File:三国名勝図会 西御在所社頭図.jpg|Kirishima Nishi Gozaisho Gongen Shrine File:三国名勝図会 雛守権現社.jpg|Hinamori Gongen Shrine File:三国名勝図会 霧島中央権現宮.jpg|Kirishima Chūō Gongen Shrine </gallery> <gallery mode="packed"> File:三国名勝図会 狭野大権現社.jpg|Sano Gongen Shrine File:三国名勝図会 霧島東御在所権現社.jpg|Kirishima Higashi Gozaisho Gongen Shrine File:三国名勝図会 東霧島権現社.jpg|Higashi Kirishima Gongen Shrine </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:霧島六社権現]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:霧島六社権現]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:霧島六社権現]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}; {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}}}}
'''Kirishima Rokusha Gongen''' (霧島六社権現) refers to a collective of six {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrines|1=Shinto shrine}} located around {{ill|Mount Kirishima|en|Mount Kirishima|WD=Q1319361|lt=Mount Kirishima|1=Mount Kirishima}}, straddling the border between {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}} and {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}. It is also known as '''Kirishima Rokusho Gongen''' (霧島六所権現).
* {{ill|Kirishima Mine Shrine|ja|霧島岑神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirishima Mine Shrine|1=Kirishimamine Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660876}} (Kirishima Mine Central Rokusho Gongen; affiliated temple: Setaoji) – Located in {{ill|Kobayashi, Miyazaki|en|Kobayashi, Miyazaki|WD=Q861283|lt=Kobayashi, Miyazaki|1=Kobayashi, Miyazaki}}, formerly a prefectural shrine. * '''Ebisumori Shrine''' (Ebisumori Rokusho Gongen; affiliated temple: {{ill|Hōkō-in (Kobayashi)|ja|宝光院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōkō-in (Kobayashi)|1=houkouin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452337}}) – Also in Kobayashi, Miyazaki; merged with Kirishima Mine Shrine during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}. * {{ill|Sano Shrine|ja|狭野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Shrine|qq=}} (Sano Gongen; affiliated temple: Jintoku-in) – Located in {{ill|Takaharu, Miyazaki|en|Takaharu, Miyazaki|WD=Q1206617|lt=Takaharu, Miyazaki|1=Takaharu, Miyazaki}}, formerly a prefectural shrine. Designated as a branch shrine of {{ill|Miyazaki Jingū|en|Miyazaki Jingū|WD=Q704686|lt=Miyazaki Jingū|1=Miyazaki Jingū}} in 1915, later regaining independence and listed as a special shrine under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}} in 1976. * Higashi Kirishima Shrine (Higashi Kirishima Gongen; affiliated temple: Chokushō-in) – Located in {{ill|Miyakonojō|en|Miyakonojō|WD=Q638301|lt=Miyakonojo, Miyazaki|1=Miyakonojō}}, formerly a prefectural shrine. * Kirishima Higashi Shrine (Kirishima Higashi Gozaisho Gongen; affiliated temple: Shakujō-in) – Located in Takaharu, Miyazaki; formerly a village shrine. * {{ill|Kirishima Shrine|en|Kirishima Shrine|WD=Q704622|lt=Kirishima Shrine|1=Kirishima Jingū}} (Kirishima Nishi Gozaisho Gongen; affiliated temple: Karin-ji) – Located in {{ill|Kirishima, Kagoshima|en|Kirishima, Kagoshima|WD=Q858352|lt=Kirishima, Kagoshima|1=Kirishima, Kagoshima}}, formerly an imperial shrine.
The Kirishima Rokusha Gongen was organized by the monk {{ill|Shōkū|ja|性空|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōkū|1=Shōkū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11491623}}, who practiced {{ill|Shugendō|en|Shugendō|WD=Q1483875|lt=Shugendō|1=Shugendō}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Murakami|en|Emperor Murakami|WD=Q374015|lt=Emperor Murakami|1=Emperor Murakami}}. Although these shrines currently enshrine deities from Japanese mythology, particularly the {{ill|Three Generations of Hyuga|ja|日向三代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Generations of Hyuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Three generations of Hyuga|WD=Q56347075}}, their origins lie in ancient {{ill|Mountain worship|en|Mountain worship|WD=Q93697439|lt=mountain worship|1=Mountain worship}}, with Mount Kirishima itself revered as sacred. The area served as a center for Shugendō practitioners.
In addition to the six shrines listed above, some sources include {{ill|Shiratori Shrine (Ebino)|ja|白鳥神社 (えびの市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratori Shrine (Ebino)|1=Shiratori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580591}} in {{ill|Ebino, Miyazaki|en|Ebino, Miyazaki|WD=Q374701|lt=Ebino, Miyazaki|1=Ebino, Miyazaki}}. The ''{{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangoku Meishō Zue|1=Sangoku meisho zue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11354726}}'' mentions '''Hanamai Rokusho Gongen''' in {{ill|Miyakonojō|en|Miyakonojō|WD=Q638301|lt=Miyakonojo|1=Miyakonojō}} and '''Yasuhara Kirishima Daigongen (Naka Kirishima Gongen)''' as part of the Kirishima Rokusha Gongen. This suggests that the grouping may have varied over time, reflecting the prominence of different shrines around Mount Kirishima<ref>Akihiko Kuroiwa, "Kirishima Rokusho Gongen" in ''Miyazaki Prefecture Shrine Records,'' Miyazaki Prefectural Shrine Office, 1988.</ref>.
== Gallery == (From ''Sangoku Meishō Zue'') <gallery mode="packed"> File:三国名勝図会 西御在所社頭図.jpg|Kirishima Nishi Gozaisho Gongen Shrine File:三国名勝図会 雛守権現社.jpg|Hinamori Gongen Shrine File:三国名勝図会 霧島中央権現宮.jpg|Kirishima Chūō Gongen Shrine </gallery> <gallery mode="packed"> File:三国名勝図会 狭野大権現社.jpg|Sano Gongen Shrine File:三国名勝図会 霧島東御在所権現社.jpg|Kirishima Higashi Gozaisho Gongen Shrine File:三国名勝図会 東霧島権現社.jpg|Higashi Kirishima Gongen Shrine </gallery>
